perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall inform its Associates of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and shall instruct them to treat the information confidentially. The receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Associates and agrees, at its sole expense, to take all commercially reasonable measures to restrain its Associates from disclosure or use of the Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall inform its personnel of the need to protect data.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the responsibility of Associates. The changed text replaces 'Associates' with 'personnel' and removes the explicit mention of commercially reasonable measures to restrain Associates. This alteration creates ambiguity, because it's now only personnel, not associates, and the commercially reasonable measures are not mentioned, weakening the responsibility for breaches. The contradiction arises because the contract elsewhere refers to 'Associates' and the responsibility for their actions regarding confidential information, which is not addressed in the modified section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party shall inform its personnel of the need to protect data. Each party agrees that it will take commercially reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of, and to avoid having Confidential Information of the other party enter the public domain, become publicly available, or be held by persons not authorized hereunder to have such Confidential Information, which measures shall include at least the same degree of care that the receiving party utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Each party agrees to notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party that may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly mentions 'Associates' and the 'commercially reasonable measures' to restrain them. The changed text replaces 'Associates' with 'personnel' and removes the explicit mention of commercially reasonable measures. This creates a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'Associates', but the 'Changed Text' does not mention them, weakening the responsibility for breaches by Associates. This creates a strong and impactful contradiction, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: The existence and the terms of this Agreement, any Transaction, the fact that any Confidential Information has been provided to the other party, and the existence, nature and status of any discussions between the parties shall be treated as Confidential Information hereunder, shall be maintained in strict confidence by the parties hereto and by their respective Associates and, subject to Section 3 of this Agreement, shall not be disclosed to any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of this Agreement shall be treated with reasonable confidentiality by the parties and their personnel.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifically includes the 'existence' of the agreement, the 'fact that any Confidential Information has been provided,' and the 'existence, nature, and status of any discussions' as Confidential Information. The modified text omits these specific details and only mandates 'reasonable confidentiality' regarding 'the terms of this Agreement'. This weakening of confidentiality creates an in-text contradiction with Section 3, which outlines 'Required Disclosure' scenarios, because it's not clear what needs to be considered under Required Disclosure, since the details were omitted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a receiving party or its Associates is or becomes legally compelled under applicable law, regulation or securities exchange listing agreement, or by a competent governmental, administrative or regulatory authority or in a proceeding before a court, arbitrator or administrative agency, whether or not as a result of a Transaction to which the Company has consented, to disclose any portion of the Confidential Information of a disclosing party, that discussions or negotiations between the parties hereto are taking place or have taken place, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to a Transaction, including the status thereof, the receiving party will, and will direct its Associates to, provide the disclosing party with prompt written notice (unless prohibited by law) of such legal compulsion, and shall delay disclosure, if and to the extent permitted or practicable, until the disclaiming party has had an opportunity to seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or to waive compliance by the receiving party and/or its Associates with the relevant provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the confidentiality requirements by omitting specific details such as the 'existence' of the agreement, the 'fact that any Confidential Information has been provided,' and the 'existence, nature, and status of any discussions.' This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which outlines 'Required Disclosure' scenarios. The changed text's omission of specific details makes it unclear what falls under the umbrella of 'Confidential Information' for the purpose of the 'Required Disclosure' clause, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Following written notice by one party to another of the termination of discussions between the parties, upon written request of the disclosing party, the receiving party shall take reasonable steps to instruct all persons involved in the Transaction to destroy all Confidential Information furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the receiving party and its Associates may retain any Confidential Information to the extent required pursuant to the regulatory compliance or record retention policies of such receiving party or Associate.
perturbation.changed_text: Following termination, the receiving party shall destroy all data. No data retention is permitted.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the clause allowing retention of Confidential Information for regulatory compliance or record retention purposes creates a direct contradiction. The original text allows for such retention, while the modified text explicitly prohibits it. Since 'regulatory compliance or record retention policies' are standard, this contradiction makes the modified section unenforceable. Also, removing the 'reasonable steps' wording makes the destruction obligation less clear. There is a contradiction, because the text makes no data retention possible, which creates an in-text violation in case there is something that should be kept for that purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Following termination, the receiving party shall destroy all data. No data retention is permitted.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, 'Following termination, the receiving party shall destroy all data. No data retention is permitted,' directly contradicts the original text by omitting the clause that allowed data retention for regulatory compliance or record retention purposes. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The original text acknowledges exceptions to data destruction, which are now completely removed in the changed text. This is a strong in-text contradiction because the changed section mandates complete data destruction, making it impossible to comply with regulatory or record retention requirements, thus contradicting the original intent and creating unenforceability issues. The explanation also highlights that the 'reasonable steps' wording makes the destruction obligation less clear. Since the change now makes no data retention possible, it creates a contradiction within the contract.

perturbation.original_text: The foregoing commitments of either party in this Agreement regarding the confidentiality and non-use of Confidential Information shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties and shall continue for a period of eighteen (18) months following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies an 18-month duration for confidentiality commitments post-termination. The modified text removes this explicit timeframe, implying perpetual confidentiality. This creates an in-text contradiction. Since it doesn't mention any timeframe, which creates an uncertain enforcement and there is no way to tell when confidentiality will end, which contradicts its own text, as it can't define at all the end of any compromisse.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies an 18-month duration for confidentiality post-termination, whereas the changed text eliminates this timeframe, implying perpetual confidentiality. The contradicted text doesn't specify the length either, however, the original text helps define the length and ending of the commitment. Since the changed text does not, it creates an uncertain enforcement, as there is no way to tell when confidentiality will end. Therefore, the change causes an in-text contradiction as it is unclear when the agreement ends.
